hero.subtitle: CREDDA and its Environmental Law Clinic work for access to justice, democracy and sustainable development in Africa — from Goma, for the entire DRC.

about.title: Defining the Future of Legal Research
about.description_p1: The Center for Research on Democracy and Development in Africa (CREDDA) is an institution of excellence dedicated to the rigorous analysis of legal and social challenges.
about.description_p2: Affiliated with ULPGL-Goma, we combine academic expertise and field action to promote equitable and sustainable justice across the continent.

research.title: Featured Research
research.subtitle: & Scientific Publications
research.description: Our research axes address governance challenges in Africa.

clinical.title: Legal Clinic for
clinical.subtitle: Environmental Protection
clinical.description: We carry the voice of communities and nature before legal authorities. Our mobile clinics ensure a continuous field presence.

legal.title: Legal Aid
legal.desc: Mediation and defense of the rights of the vulnerable.

land.title: Land Justice
land.desc: Securing the rights of local and indigenous communities.

mobile.title: Mobile Clinics
mobile.desc: Free legal assistance in the heart of vulnerable areas.

items.name: David MICHAEL PEYTON
items.text: I could not have asked for more ideal research partners than the faculty and staff at CREDDA-ULPGL. They were not only able to support multiple types of data collection but also provided opportunities for feedback from Congolese academics.

items.name: Heather LYNNE ZIMMERMAN
items.role: Masters student, London School of Economics (LSE)
items.text: Dear Professor Kennedy Kihangi, thank you very much for generously welcoming me into the community! I am grateful for the ideas and feedback offered on my research. I am eager to return to Goma.

items.name: Britta Sjöstedt
items.text: I visited ULPGL in 2015 to conduct research for my PhD. Professor Kennedy KIHANGI BINDU was an excellent host that helped to get in contact with other researchers and organisations.

library.title: Digital Library
library.description: Discover our latest research reports, legal analyses and case studies.

Gallery.description: Discover our research activities, clinic works and collaborations through a selection of photos.

video.title: Discover the <primary>Impact</primary> of CREDDA
video.description: Dive into the heart of our initiatives through this presentation. We work for academic excellence and social innovation in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

hero.title: Thinking <italic>Law</italic>, Building the Future.
hero.desc: CREDDA is the epicenter of legal research in the DRC, merging scientific rigor and social engagement.

about.mission_description: CREDDA is not just a think tank. It is the scientific arm of ULPGL that materializes the link between legal theories and field realities in the DRC.
achievements: Free legal aid for the vulnerable
Democracy observatory in the Great Lakes
about.mission_vision_title: A clear mission for a resilient Africa.

mission.title: A <italic>transformative</italic> vision of law.
mission.desc: Beyond texts, we believe in living law. A law that protects the Congo Basin ecosystems and guarantees the dignity of indigenous peoples. CREDDA is not just a laboratory, it is a legal shield.

about.mission_text: Produce high-quality research to guide public policies and strengthen the rule of law in the DRC.
about.vision_text: Become the main interdisciplinary hub for conflict transformation in Central Africa.
about.values_text: Academic integrity, political neutrality, and commitment to the rights of vulnerable communities.
about.location_description: CREDDA's headquarters is located within the Salomon Campus of ULPGL, a privileged academic setting in the heart of Goma.
full_address: Université Libre des Pays des Grands Lacs (ULPGL),
Salomon Campus, Avenue de la Corniche,
about.quote: Law must not only be spoken, it must be lived in the service of climate and social justice.

pillars.title: The Legal Pillars
pillars.desc: Our actions are strictly in line with national and international normative frameworks.

header.title: Environmental Law Clinic
header.description: An institution of excellence within CREDDA-ULPGL dedicated to environmental protection and rights of local communities.

intro.title: Context and Justification
intro.text: The launch of Environmental Law Clinic (CDE) responds to urgent need to provide practical solutions for social justice and access to justice, constitutional right recognized for indigenous pygmy peoples and local communities in the DRC.

mission.title: Our Mission
items: Legal advice and guidance for litigants
Mobile clinics and legal awareness campaigns

axes.title: Capacity Building
axes.desc: Training students, judges and community leaders.

axes.title: Advocacy
axes.desc: Influence environmental laws and public policies.

cta.title: Report an environmental injustice
cta.description: Are you a victim of pollution, land conflict or deforestation? We can help you.

Track.title: Track Your Case.
Track.description: Enter your registered phone number to access your status and follow the progression of your legal registry.
Track.no_records_desc: We couldn't find any clinical cases associated with this phone number. Please check the number or contact our office.
Track.confidentiality_desc: Your data is protected by judicial confidentiality. Access is restricted to registered beneficiaries.
Track.authority_desc: Statuses are updated in real-time by our legal experts after each procedural step.

Search.footer_hint: Press Enter to see all results

ClinicalMap.title: Field Presence
ClinicalMap.description: CREDDA deploys its experts as close as possible to the communities. Our fixed and mobile clinics cover strategic points in the Great Lakes region.

header.title: Privacy Policy
header.description: CREDDA-ULPGL is committed to protecting your personal information in accordance with ethical and academic standards.

sections.title: Data Collected
sections.content: We collect data provided via our forms (contact, registration, clinical tracking): name, email, phone, and institutional affiliation.

sections.title: Processing Purposes
sections.content: Your data is used for tracking legal clinic cases, distributing our publications, and managing our researcher network.

sections.title: Security & Retention
sections.content: Data is encrypted and stored on secure servers. Access is strictly restricted to the scientific secretariat.

sections.title: Your Rights
sections.content: You have the right to access, rectify and delete your data upon request to privacy@credda-ulpgl.org.

footer.title: Need more information?
footer.description: For any questions regarding our privacy policy or to exercise your rights, please do not hesitate to contact us.

header.title: Legal Mentions
header.description: Legal information regarding the institution and site terms of use.

sections.title: Site Publisher
sections.content: Center for Research on Democracy and Development in Africa (CREDDA-ULPGL), an academic institution of the Free University of the Great Lakes Countries (ULPGL-Goma).

sections.title: Management
sections.content: Publisher: Prof. Dr. Kennedy Kihangi Bindu, Director of CREDDA.

sections.title: Headquarters
sections.content: Salomon Campus, Avenue de la Corniche, Q. Himbi, Goma, North Kivu, Democratic Republic of the Congo.

sections.title: Intellectual Property
sections.content: All reports, articles and multimedia content are the exclusive property of CREDDA-ULPGL, unless otherwise stated. Any reproduction is subject to prior authorization.
